project.label: Compounding Quality RAG
project.summary: A local-first, human-in-the-loop review-support system combining a React workflow, Spring Boot service boundary, and Python RAG engine. It emphasizes strict contracts, deterministic safety policy, grounded LLM extraction, and cross-service traceability.

files.title: Review Pipeline Orchestrator
files.responsibility: Centralizes validation, refusal screening, collaborator injection, and stage ordering for checklist, retrieval, extraction, and final assessment across every transport adapter.
files.interviewLine: I centralized review sequencing here so HTTP, CLI, and bridge adapters cannot drift on validation, refusal, or stage order.

files.title: Canonical Domain Schemas
files.responsibility: Defines strict Pydantic contracts, domain enums, nested review outputs, and cross-field invariants that reject unknown fields and invalid classification or resolution combinations.
files.interviewLine: I use strict schemas and cross-field validators to make invalid domain states and silent contract drift fail immediately.

files.title: Retrieval Query Strategies
files.responsibility: Implements interchangeable query-construction strategies, semantic-intent derivation, deterministic expansion, validated query results, and a versioned cache keyed by content and model identity.
files.interviewLine: I separated query interpretation behind strategy objects so retrieval approaches can be compared without changing the retriever itself.

files.title: Grounded Summary Extraction
files.responsibility: Converts LLM JSON into validated review summaries, normalizes payloads, applies deterministic evidence grounding, detects negation and support, and isolates the model behind a narrow protocol.
files.interviewLine: I treat the model as an untrusted proposer, then validate, normalize, and ground every structured finding against the pharmacist's narrative.

files.title: Final Assessment Policy
files.responsibility: Maps checklist evidence and reviewer-confirmed structured findings into deterministic risk lanes, classifications, scopes, escalation paths, resolution options, and rationale.
files.interviewLine: I keep final disposition deterministic and driven by reviewer-confirmed triggers rather than letting raw model text make escalation decisions.

files.title: FastAPI Transport Boundary
files.responsibility: Exposes validated HTTP endpoints, delegates all domain sequencing to the review pipeline, translates domain failures into status codes, reports readiness, and propagates request correlation.
files.interviewLine: This adapter owns HTTP concerns only and delegates review behavior to the shared pipeline, preserving one domain path across transports.

files.title: Python Engine HTTP Adapter
files.responsibility: Implements the Java-to-Python port with explicit request records, snake-case wire mapping, bounded timeouts, request-ID forwarding, health probing, response decoding, and typed exception translation.
files.interviewLine: I isolated the cross-language HTTP contract here, including wire-name translation, timeouts, correlation headers, and typed failure handling.

files.title: RAG Wire Contract Tests
files.responsibility: Drives the real Java adapter against an in-process capturing HTTP stub and asserts exact Python-facing JSON keys for all four cross-language RPC requests.
files.interviewLine: These tests pin the exact JSON wire names so independent Java and Python refactors cannot silently break their shared contract.

files.title: Review Workflow State Machine
files.responsibility: Owns typed asynchronous states and transitions for checklist, retrieval, extraction, and final assessment, including invalidation, retry, reset, demo loading, and backend-readiness guards.
files.interviewLine: I modeled the UI workflow as explicit typed states and transitions so stale downstream results are invalidated when upstream inputs change.

files.title: Health-Gated Service Composition
files.responsibility: Composes the UI, Spring API, and Python engine on one network with explicit environment configuration, service-specific health checks, and dependency startup gated on healthy upstream services.
files.interviewLine: I use health-gated composition so dependent services start only after their upstream boundary is actually responsive.

concepts.claim: The run_* entry points form an application-service seam that coordinates domain collaborators while keeping transport-specific status codes and response shapes outside the module.
Pipeline functions accept callable collaborators with production defaults, allowing deterministic tests and adapter-specific resource wiring without global mutation.
_screened_text rejects blank input and invalid top_k before executing retrieval or assessment work, while extraction explicitly validates malformed input before refusal screening.
The module applies the refusal boundary from one shared location, preventing HTTP, CLI, and bridge entry points from implementing inconsistent safety policy.
StrictBaseModel forbids unknown fields, Field constraints validate local values, and model validators enforce relationships spanning multiple fields.
Enums and nested Pydantic models represent intake, product context, investigation requirements, reviewer findings, and derived assessment as explicit domain vocabulary.
Validators require QRE category data, forbid category data for general questions, and reject resolution options when resolution review is false.
Raw, deterministic-expansion, rule-intent, and Nano-intent implementations share the RetrievalQueryStrategy build contract while varying query construction independently.
RetrievalQueryStrategy defines the minimal behavioral contract needed by callers, allowing implementations to vary without inheritance or retriever changes.
RetrievalIntentCache keys entries using question identity, input content, model identity, and schema version, preventing stale semantic-intent reuse across meaningful changes.
The extraction path parses model JSON, normalizes fields, validates against ReviewSummary, and raises domain-specific errors when the output violates the contract.
Model-proposed findings are passed through deterministic grounding that checks narrative support, negation, and evidence status before returning structured output.
LLMClient is a narrow protocol that keeps provider SDK behavior outside the domain extraction logic and enables network-free tests.
Risk lane, review scope, handling path, escalation, and resolution options are selected by explicit code branches over structured checklist and reviewer-summary values.
Severe escalation depends on review_summary.severe_triggers_observed, making reviewer-confirmed structured findings the decision input rather than raw model text alone.
FastAPI handlers validate transport models, invoke shared pipeline functions, and convert domain outcomes to HTTP responses without reimplementing review sequencing.
Handlers map ReviewRefused, ValueError, LLM client failures, and unexpected exceptions to distinct HTTP status categories at the service boundary.
HTTP middleware reads X-Request-Id, logs it with method, path, and status, then echoes it on the response.
HttpRagEngineClient implements RagEngineClient while encapsulating Java HTTP, JSON serialization, Python endpoint paths, and engine-specific error handling.
Private request records and JsonProperty annotations explicitly translate Java names to the snake_case contract expected by strict Python request models.
Separate request and health-probe clients use bounded connect and per-request timeouts, reflecting different latency expectations for work and liveness checks.
An in-process HTTP stub captures real adapter requests and assertions pin required snake_case keys while rejecting leaked camelCase names.
The capture helper creates a focused HTTP stub that records outbound bodies, allowing the production client to be tested without a running Python service.
The hook represents each stage with idle, loading, success, or error states and defines explicit transitions for submit, retry, reset, and upstream edits.
RequestState<T> uses the status discriminator so success data and error details are available only in their valid states.
Editing pharmacist notes after successful extraction clears extraction and final-assessment state, preventing stale downstream conclusions from remaining visible.
Compose defines three independently built services, one shared network, and explicit service dependencies matching the runtime architecture.
Each service has a concrete health command, and dependent services use service_healthy conditions rather than relying only on process startup.
The API waits for the engine health check, and the UI waits for the API health check, encoding startup order from actual service readiness.
